question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user poison the LRU entityCache by submitting a crafted /block request that causes a malicious accountId to be cached under a victim's GetId() key, so that subsequent operations on the victim's account resolve to the attacker's alias, effectively authorizing operations under the victim's identity without their approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of cache invalidation in entityCache to permanently substitute a victim's AccountId with an attacker-controlled alias, causing all future block operations to attribute transactions to the wrong signer and bypass owner approval checks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a race condition between concurrent /block requests that both call entityCache.Set(result.GetId(), result) for the same account, causing one request's alias to overwrite another's and authorize a transaction under a signer who never approved it?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a BlockRequest with a PartialBlockIdentifier that resolves to a block containing another user's transactions, then use the returned Operations to replay those transactions without the original signer's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the lack of authentication on the /block endpoint to enumerate all transactions in a block, extract signer AccountIds from Operations, and use those identifiers to construct unauthorized transactions impersonating those signers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user abuse the maxTransactionsInBlock truncation logic to cause the OtherTransactions list to omit certain transactions from the response, hiding unauthorized transactions from block auditors while they are still executed on-chain?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user call /block/transaction with an arbitrary TransactionIdentifier.Hash and a valid BlockIdentifier to retrieve any transaction's Operations and AccountIds without proving ownership, then use those details to forge a matching unsigned transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a BlockIdentifier.Hash with or without the 0x prefix (exploiting SafeRemoveHexPrefix) to bypass block hash validation and retrieve transactions from an unintended block, exposing signer identities that can be used to authorize transactions without owner consent?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user call /block/transaction with a mismatched BlockIdentifier.Index and BlockIdentifier.Hash pair to cause FindByIdentifier to return a block whose consensus range is used to look up a transaction hash that belongs to a different block, leaking cross-block signer information?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user trigger GetAccountAlias on a crafted accountId whose GetId() collides with a legitimate account's encoded ID in the entityCache, causing the cache to return the attacker's alias for the victim's account and authorizing operations under the victim's identity?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: NewBlockAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exhaust the LRU entityCache capacity (entityCacheConfig.MaxSize) by flooding /block requests with unique accountIds, evicting legitimate cached entries and forcing repeated GetAccountAlias lookups that can be manipulated via a timing attack to substitute signer identities?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with a PartialBlockIdentifier where both Hash and Index are nil, causing RetrieveLatest to return the most recent block, and then use the returned transaction Operations to identify current active signers without any authorization check?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /block request with only a Hash (no Index) to trigger FindByHash, and by brute-forcing or guessing block hashes, enumerate all historical transactions and their associated signer AccountIds to build a target list for unauthorized transaction construction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that entityCache.Set stores result.GetId() as the key (not the original accountId.GetId()) to insert a mapping where a different numeric ID maps to an attacker-controlled alias, causing future operations to resolve the wrong signer for authorization?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a /block request that returns a block with exactly maxTransactionsInBlock+1 transactions, causing the last transaction to be moved to OtherTransactions, and then use /block/transaction to fetch that transaction's signer details without the alias-update step being applied, leaking raw AccountIds?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a transaction hash that matches a transaction in a different block's consensus range than the one specified by BlockIdentifier, exploiting FindByHashInBlock's range parameters to retrieve a transaction whose signer never authorized it for that block context?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a nil-pointer dereference or incorrect alias resolution by submitting a /block/transaction request for a transaction whose Operations contain an AccountId with GetId() == 0, bypassing the cache lookup and triggering GetAccountAlias with a zero-value account that returns an attacker-controlled result?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the sequential processing of block.Transactions in updateOperationAccountAlias to perform a cache-poisoning attack mid-iteration, where a concurrent request sets a malicious alias for an account being processed, causing the victim's operation to be attributed to the attacker's account?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user call /block/transaction with a valid block hash but a transaction hash that belongs to a transaction the caller does not own, and since there is no ownership check in FindByHashInBlock, retrieve the full operation details including signer AccountIds to impersonate the original signer?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the in-place mutation of operations[index].AccountId inside updateOperationAccountAlias to cause a shared Transaction object (if cached at the repository layer) to have its AccountId permanently replaced with an attacker-controlled alias, affecting all future consumers of that transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user send repeated /block requests for the same block to cause the entityCache to be populated with aliases resolved from a compromised or delayed GetAccountAlias response, effectively authorizing future transactions under stale or incorrect signer identities?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: NewBlockAPIService()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the shared serverContext-bound entityCache across all requests to inject a crafted alias entry that persists for the lifetime of the server, permanently associating a victim's numeric account ID with an attacker-controlled alias for all subsequent transaction authorization checks?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: Block()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that FindBetween returns all transactions between ConsensusStartNanos and ConsensusEndNanos without any caller-identity check, to harvest the full set of signer AccountIds for a given time range and use them to construct transactions that appear authorized by those signers?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: BlockTransaction()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the two-step lookup (FindByIdentifier then FindByHashInBlock) in BlockTransaction to perform a TOCTOU attack where the block's consensus range changes between the two calls, causing a transaction from a different block to be returned and its signer details to be used without that signer's approval?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/services/block_service.go -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: updateOperationAccountAlias()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a request that causes GetAccountAlias to return an AccountId with a GetId() value that collides with a high-value account's encoded ID, poisoning the entityCache and causing all subsequent operations for that high-value account to be authorized under the attacker's alias?
